verdicts.reason: All specified correctness, boundary, and error handling requirements for the Command Address Book feature are met.
The Command Address Book feature does not introduce new exploitable attack surfaces, adhering to existing CSP and using safe DOM manipulation methods for user-controlled input.
All specified UI and accessibility requirements for the Command Address Book feature have been met, including font sizes, aria-labels, mutual drawer exclusion, and clear inline error messages.
The Command Address Book feature is highly discoverable with clear labeling, actionable error messages, helpful placeholders, and explicit AI agent documentation.
The Command Address Book directly solves the problem of repetitive typing and ensures consistency for frequently used command addresses, enhancing speed and accuracy for the target user.
verdicts.evidence: This feature is a clear tool, not a toy, for naval personnel drafting formal correspondence. It directly supports the project's utility-flagship identity by streamlining a common, repetitive task (filling From/To/Via fields) that is not adequately handled by generic browser autofill or external lookups.

No documented lessons or architectural constraints were violated by the Command Address Book feature. All specific requirements for the TESTS gate were met.
